prs.title: fix: update Stadia source URL
prs.body:
Issue: closes #11901


Checklist
[x] I have reviewed the [forbidden brands](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#forbidden-brands) list and confirm the brand I am submitting a PR for is not one of them, nor is it a subsidiary of one of those brands

[***] I have reviewed the brand's terms of service, and am confident we can add this icon

[***] I updated the JSON data in data/simple-icons.json

[] I optimized the icon with SVGO or SVGOMG (Leave unchecked, you didn't change the SVG)

[] The SVG viewbox is 0 0 24 24 (Leave unchecked, you didn't change the SVG)

Description
The original source link for Stadia (https://stadia.google.com/home) is dead following the service shutdown in January 2023.

I have updated the source to the Wikimedia Commons file (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Stadia_logo.svg) to ensure the logo source remains accessible.

prs.title: fix(shiki): rename `createdBundledHighlighter` to `createBundledHighlighter`
prs.body:
### Description

This PR renames createdBundledHighlighter to createBundledHighlighter to improve naming consistency across the codebase (aligning with other factory functions like createHighlighter).

To ensure backward compatibility and avoid breaking changes, createdBundledHighlighter has been re-exported as a deprecated alias pointing to the new function.

Internal usages in bundle-full.ts and bundle-web.ts have been updated to use the new name.

### Linked Issues
Fixes #1076

### Additional context
** Ran pnpm run build and verified the project builds successfully.

** Ran pnpm test and verified all tests passed.

** The deprecation ensures this is a non-breaking change for existing users.

prs.title: fix(xhr): clamp upload progress loaded value to total
prs.body:
Fixes an issue where XHR implementations (like in React Native) can report a `loaded` value greater than `total`, causing progress calculations to exceed 100%.

This change adds a check to clamp `e.loaded` to `e.total` if it exceeds the total, ensuring the progress event remains valid.

Fixes #7232

prs.title: fix: preserve case in setCharset
prs.body:
<!--
Thank you for your pull request. Please provide a description and 
note the Certificate of Origin below. 

-->
This change fixes an issue where `utils.setCharset` would incorrectly convert the content type to lowercase when adding a charset.

This PR preserves the original case of the content type, as discussed in the issue. It also includes a new test case to verify this behaviour.

Fixes #6613

prs.title: feat: Implement Landing Page UI, Styling, and basic search state
prs.body:
### Summary of Changes

This Pull Request establishes the **visual structure and frontend scaffolding** for the application's main landing page, significantly advancing the Web UI component of the project.

### What Was Done

-   **Implemented Full Landing Page UI:** Created the primary structure using semantic HTML within `App.tsx`, including a hero section, a dedicated search area, and a call-to-action section.
-   **Introduced Styling (CSS):** Added comprehensive CSS to `App.css` to achieve the final, visually polished look. The styling is fully responsive for mobile devices.
-   **Prepared for Search Functionality:** Integrated React **`useState`** to handle the `searchQuery` input value and the `loading` state for the search button. This sets up the **data flow** for future API integration.


### Why This Change is Important

-   **Visual Identity & Structure:** Delivers the complete frontend appearance and establishes the basic layout for all subsequent features.
-   **Functionality Scaffolding:** Provides the necessary state management and component structure where the core logic (API calls to `/search`) will be implemented in a future PR.





This is a continued contribution for **#Hacktoberfest**. 🎃

prs.title: feat: Add Header, Footer, and GreetUser components to app layout
prs.body:
Summary of Changes
This Pull Request adds the foundational layout components for the application, resolving critical import errors in the main application file.

It fixes a bug where App.tsx was attempting to import Header, Footer, and GreetUser components that did not yet exist in the codebase.

What Was Added
New Component: Header.tsx: Provides the main site title and navigation structure.
New Component: Footer.tsx: Provides copyright and general site information at the bottom of the page.
New Component: GreetUser.tsx: A basic component to demonstrate prop handling and display a welcome message.
Modified: App.tsx: Imported and rendered the new components to complete the initial page layout.
Proof of Work
The application now builds successfully and runs locally without errors, displaying the new Header and Footer components.

This is a contribution for #Hacktoberfest. 🎃

prs.title: feat: Add Header, Footer, and GreetUser components to app layout
prs.body:
### Summary of Changes

This Pull Request adds the foundational layout components for the application, resolving critical import errors in the main application file.

It fixes a bug where `App.tsx` was attempting to import `Header`, `Footer`, and `GreetUser` components that did not yet exist in the codebase.

### What Was Added

-   **New Component: `Header.tsx`**: Provides the main site title and navigation structure.
-   **New Component: `Footer.tsx`**: Provides copyright and general site information at the bottom of the page.
-   **New Component: `GreetUser.tsx`**: A basic component to demonstrate prop handling and display a welcome message.
-   **Modified: `App.tsx`**: Imported and rendered the new components to complete the initial page layout.

### Proof of Work

The application now builds successfully and runs locally without errors, displaying the new Header and Footer components.

---

**This is a contribution for #Hacktoberfest.** 🎃
